name: Moneytree
description_short: Moneytree offers payday and installment loans up to $700, plus check cashing and wire transfers at physical locations in Washington state.
description_long: Moneytree is a licensed consumer finance company operating physical branch locations across Washington state, including the Burien branch at 116 SW 148th St. The company has been providing short-term lending and financial services to consumers seeking immediate access to cash. With a 4 out of 5 star rating, the Burien location operates extended hours Monday through Friday (9 AM–7 PM) and Saturday (10 AM–5 PM), positioning itself as an accessible option for working consumers. The company is subject to Washington state lending regulations and maintains borrower eligibility checks through state databases. Moneytree's primary offerings include payday loans (up to $700), installment loans (up to $700), and supplementary financial services. Payday loans are flat-fee products charging $15 per $100 borrowed up to $500, then $10 per $100 over $500—translating to a 391.07% APR on a 14-day $100 loan. Beyond lending, the company offers check cashing (consumer and business), prepaid cards, and wire transfers. Funds can be received in cash, via bank ACH transfer (online loans only), or through Instant Funding to qualified debit cards within 30–45 minutes. Moneytree's competitive positioning relies on speed, physical accessibility, and straightforward flat-fee pricing rather than complexity. The company explicitly discloses APR calculations and provides sample loan documents, enabling borrowers to understand costs upfront. The extended business hours and multiple payment receipt methods accommodate various consumer schedules and banking situations. The company also refuses loans to military-covered borrowers under the Military Lending Act, demonstrating regulatory compliance awareness. However, Moneytree's products carry significant financial costs typical of high-APR lending. A 391% APR on payday loans far exceeds traditional credit products and can create debt cycles for consumers who repeatedly borrow. The $700 loan caps and short terms suit emergency gaps but not sustained financial needs. The company's customer notice acknowledges that small-dollar loans "used over a long period of time can be expensive," suggesting awareness of potential consumer harm. This is genuinely suited only for one-time cash emergencies, not recurring financial solutions.
address: 116 SW 148th St Suite D-120, Burien, WA 98166

company_info.headquarters: 116 SW 148th St Suite D-120, Burien, WA 98166

pros: Fast funding available within 30–45 minutes via Instant Funding to qualified debit cards
Extended business hours (9 AM–7 PM weekdays, 10 AM–5 PM Saturday) accommodate working consumers
Straightforward flat-fee pricing: $15/$100 up to $500, then $10/$100 over $500—no hidden rate structures
Multiple fund delivery methods: cash, ACH transfer (online), or debit card deposit
Transparent APR disclosure (391.07% example) and sample loan fee documents available upfront
In-person branch availability for those without digital access or preferring face-to-face service
Comprehensive supplementary services including check cashing, wire transfers, and prepaid cards
Complies with Military Lending Act, refusing loans to covered borrowers
cons: APR of 391.07% is extremely high and creates significant debt risk for repeated borrowing
Loan maximum of $700 limits usefulness for larger financial emergencies or consolidated debt
Payday loan structure (14-day lump-sum repayment) is inflexible and difficult for income-limited borrowers
Company explicitly warns that small-dollar loans "used over a long period of time can be expensive," indicating trap-like product design
Requires active checking account, proof of income, and valid ID—excludes unbanked or undocumented populations
best_for: Workers facing a one-time cash emergency (medical bill, car repair, eviction notice) who can repay within 14 days
Unbanked consumers who need check cashing or wire transfer services at a physical location
Borrowers with poor credit history unable to access traditional personal loans or credit cards
diagnosis: Moneytree is appropriate exclusively for borrowers facing genuine one-time financial emergencies who can repay within 2 weeks and have active checking accounts. The 391% APR and short repayment terms make this unsuitable for recurring or larger financial needs; repeated use creates predatory debt cycles. This is a genuinely high-cost option best avoided unless alternatives (credit unions, employer advances, family loans, non-profit assistance) are unavailable.
services: Payday loans up to $700 with 14-day repayment terms
Installment loans up to $700 with flexible 70+ day repayment schedules
Signature loans (mentioned but not detailed on this page)
Instant Funding to qualified debit cards
In-person loan applications and funding at branch locations
